name: Directional target-nomination benchmark
description: Gold-standard directional labels for immune drug targets. true_direction is derived from the mechanism of the APPROVED/clinical drug against each target (an inhibitor implies the target is a driver to be antagonized; an agonist/replacement implies a brake to be agonized). This is external ground truth INDEPENDENT of any perturbation screen, usable to benchmark whether a directional screen-scoring method calls the therapeutically correct direction.

columns.opentargets_genetic_score: OT genetic-association score (context; genetics is direction-agnostic)
columns.in_perturbseq_shortlist: whether the gene passed the Perturb-seq quality gate

honest_baseline.note: On the full 63-target set the framework's directional accuracy (0.62) is BELOW the majority-class baseline (0.90) because the set is dominated by antagonize targets; balanced accuracy ~0.57, permutation p~0.30. This benchmark is released WITH this honest negative so others can measure directional resolution on their own screens rather than assume it.

citation: Perturb2Target (Claude Science hackathon). Directional labels curated from approved-drug mechanisms; source screen Zhu, Dann et al. 2025 bioRxiv 10.64898/2025.12.23.696273.
